name: Quarry
notes: Canonical stone dropoff. Fortification fuel line begins here.

name: Iron Mine
notes: Canonical iron production with smelting chain.
Iron delivery consumes wood as smelting fuel at ratio 0.5 wood per iron.
If wood is insufficient, iron is returned to node.

name: Stable
notes: Canonical cavalry seat for stage 2 warfare.
Scout Riders require Stable plus iron access.

name: Siege Workshop
notes: Dedicated siege infrastructure required for serious formal assaults.
Ram, Siege Tower, Trebuchet, Engineer, and Supply Wagon production now routes through this workshop instead of the Barracks.

name: Supply Camp
notes: Canonical forward logistics seat for a formal siege line.
Supply wagons must remain linked to a live camp to keep nearby siege engines fully supplied.
Also acts as first-layer field-water anchor for campaigning land armies.

name: Wall Segment
notes: Canonical outer-works component. Structural damage multiplier of 0.2 means line infantry cannot efficiently break walls.
Wall segments adjacent to a settlement contribute to its fortification tier.

name: Watch Tower
notes: Canonical flanking tower. Sight bonus extends vision for friendly units within auraRadius.
Modest attack aura reinforces overwatch doctrine.

name: Gatehouse
notes: Canonical gatehouse with killing-bay. Only wall opening that passes friendly traffic.
Higher armor and structural multiplier than wall because gate is canonically harder to force.

name: Inner Keep
notes: Canonical primary-keep foundation. Unlocks higher fortification tier at the bloodline seat.
Structural multiplier of 0.1 makes the keep canonically resilient — even rams take meaningful time to breach.

name: Wayshrine
notes: Canonical first-tier covenant structure per master doctrine section XIX.
When built within faithExposureRadius of any sacred site, amplifies the faction's faith exposure accrual there by faithExposureAmplification.
Once the faction has committed to a covenant, Wayshrines tied to that covenant contribute to faith intensity regen, strengthening ward profiles on nearby fortifications.
Session 13 first canonical layer. L2 Hall, L3 Grand Sanctuary, L4 Apex structures remain DOCUMENTED pending further sessions.

name: Covenant Hall
notes: Session 17: canonical L2 covenant hall per master doctrine section XIX.
Requires that the faction has committed to a covenant; building fails if no doctrine path is selected.
Stronger exposure amp (2.4x vs Wayshrine 1.8x) and wider radius (240 vs 180).
When built within faith-aura of a fortified settlement owned by the same faction, adds wardSightBonusExtra and wardDefenderAttackBonusExtra on top of the covenant ward profile.
Session 20: trains all 8 canonical L3 faith units (2 per covenant per doctrine path). Training guards on committed faith + doctrine path match.
L4 Apex structures remain DOCUMENTED pending further sessions.

name: Grand Sanctuary
notes: Session 21: canonical L3 Grand Sanctuary per master doctrine section XIX.
Requires committed covenant + doctrine path (via requiresFaithCommitment gate).
Strongest-yet non-apex exposure amp (3.0x) and radius (320).
Recruitment seat for L4 canonical faith units (8 total, 2 per covenant per path).

name: Harbor
notes: Session 27: canonical L1 harbor per master doctrine section XV + 11_MATCHFLOW/NAVAL_SYSTEM.md.
Gates vessel training; must be placed adjacent to a water tile (requiresCoastalAdjacency).
Food + wood dropoff reflects canonical coastal economy (fishing + shipbuilding).
Session 28: transport_ship added to trainables.
L3 Admiralty remains DOCUMENTED pending a later session.

name: Great Harbor
notes: Session 28: canonical L2 harbor. Trains apex vessel classes (Fire Ship, Capital Ship).
Larger 4x4 footprint, stronger dropoff chain including gold (canonical naval trade pressure).
L3 Admiralty still DOCUMENTED.

name: Apex Covenant
notes: Session 22: canonical L4 Apex covenant structure per master doctrine section XIX.
Requires committed covenant + doctrine path AND faith intensity >= 80 (Fervent tier).
Trains the L5 apex unit corresponding to the faction's committed covenant (only 1 of 4 is accessible per match, enforced by faithId + doctrinePath gates).
Strongest-yet exposure amp (3.6x) and radius (400). Biggest footprint (5x5).
Anti-snowball: only reachable by a faction that has genuinely committed to and sustained its covenant. Canonical late-game divergence lock per master doctrine section XXI.
